problem: The sibling renderreport crate's dominant_issue_spotlight.typ and severity_overview.typ Typst component templates hardcoded German fallback label text (e.g. "% Anteil", "Nutzer-Wirkung", "Empfehlung", the five severity-overview labels) with no locale parameter, so any auditmysite caller passing English data through these components would still see German chrome around it.
evidence: Discovered while investigating why "Empfehlung" appeared in an EN-locale --debug-typ dump; on inspection, neither component is actually instantiated anywhere in auditmysite's own PDF-building code (grep -rn "DominantIssueSpotlight"/"SeverityOverview" src/ returned nothing) — this was dead-code hygiene, not a live user-facing bug, and was corrected as such to the user before being fixed.
expected: renderreport component templates accept locale-appropriate label overrides rather than hardcoding one language's fallback text, even for components not currently wired up by any caller, so future use doesn't silently reintroduce a locale leak.
regression: renderreport 0.2.36: DominantIssueSpotlight::with_labels(...) and SeverityOverview::with_labels(...) builder methods plus data.at("label_...", default: ...) overrides in both .typ templates.
counter_examples: A caller that does not pass label overrides still gets the original German defaults — this is an additive override, not a removal of the existing default behavior.
